mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales indicates resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is observed.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a high tightening threshold, higher discount rates reduce the present value of equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% or rises by more than 1.0pp over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate signals broad income growth without labor-market deterioration, raising expected consumption and earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 300 thousand and UNRATE 3-month change <= 0.0pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2005-11-01 was 134289 and UNRATE was 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger does not outperform cases where only PAYEMS is positive, or if triggered 20-day returns are below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A material break lower in housing starts signals weakening residential investment, construction employment, and household durables demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-start declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or have no negative return spread versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production trend indicates improving real output and capacity utilization, which supports earnings revisions for cyclical sectors and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a nonpositive coefficient for 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward regressions or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration during a rising policy-rate cycle creates margin pressure and forces tighter monetary policy, so the joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4% and FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2005-11-01 was 197.8 and FEDFUNDS was 4.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger is not followed by lower 20-day returns than either single-series trigger alone, or if triggered returns are positive over 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and recession risk, causing investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp over 3 months are not followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation acceleration above its recent trend prompts short-horizon repricing of policy-rate expectations and equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month change crosses above its trailing 12-month average monthly change
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers do not generate negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the hit rate for negative 5-day returns is below 50%.
